candidates.candidate_name: Georgetown University - Hoya Summer High School Sessions (College Prep Program)

overnight_evidence.snippet: This five-week, residential-only program for high school students allows you to take a university course while participating in College Prep Seminars.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: 2026 Season Information: Information for the upcoming summer of 2026 is available.

candidates.notes: Focuses on SAT/ACT prep and college readiness skills.

overnight_evidence.snippet: Accelerated, hands-on learning experiences... Students focus on a single subject area through collaborative work, simulations, discussions, and field trips. Includes residential options.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: Information for the upcoming summer of 2026 is available.

candidates.notes: Subjects include Law, Forensic Science, International Relations, etc.

candidates.candidate_name: George Washington University - Pre-College Summer Immersion

overnight_evidence.snippet: Students have the opportunity to live on campus, allowing them to participate in social and recreational activities while forming connections with peers from around the world.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: Check the official GW Pre-College website for the most current information for the upcoming season.

candidates.notes: Integrates lecture-based instruction with experiential learning using DC as a 'living classroom'.

candidates.candidate_name: George Washington University - Caminos al Futuro

overnight_evidence.snippet: A selective, fully-funded, three-week intensive residential program for rising high school seniors.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: Applications for future summers are typically handled via the Cisneros Institute.

candidates.notes: Focuses on leadership development for Hispanic and Latino students.

overnight_evidence.snippet: Students live in AU residence halls, dine on campus, and participate in field trips to D.C.-area government agencies, embassies, and global organizations.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: Visit the official AU Pre-College Programs website for the most current information.

candidates.notes: Hybrid format with heavy residential component.

candidates.candidate_name: Howard University - Upward Bound Summer Academy

overnight_evidence.snippet: Often includes a residential component on Howard's campus where students receive academic instruction and college preparation support.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: Information regarding summer sessions is updated annually on the Howard University website.

candidates.notes: Targeted primarily toward local DC and Prince George's County students.
validation_needs: Confirm exact duration and current residential status for 2026

candidates.candidate_name: National Student Leadership Conference (NSLC) - Washington DC Sessions

overnight_evidence.snippet: NSLC programs are primarily residential, meaning high school students are generally required to live in supervised campus dormitories.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: The website provides current information for upcoming programs and application cycles.

candidates.notes: Programs in DC include Law & Advocacy, International Diplomacy, National Security, and Intelligence.

candidates.candidate_name: Junior Statesmen of America (JSA) - Georgetown Summer School

overnight_evidence.snippet: JSA offers residential summer programs on major university campuses including Georgetown.

recent_activity_evidence.snippet: Website reflects upcoming summer leadership opportunities.

candidates.notes: Highly focused on public policy, debate, and civics.

candidates.candidate_name: Envision Experience - NYLF programs at American University

overnight_evidence.snippet: Career-focused residential programs hosted on university campuses.

candidates.notes: National Youth Leadership Forums (NYLF) are a major part of their offering in DC.

candidates.candidate_name: Catholic University of America - Experiences in Architecture (EiA)

overnight_evidence.snippet: The program typically offers both residential and commuting options. Dates: July 6 - July 17, 2026. Room & Board fee listed.

candidates.notes: Intense pre-college workshop for rising 10th-12th graders.
